items.title: Arrive Punta Cana Airport (PUJ)
items.detail: Flight arrives at PUJ Terminal A. Taxi or shuttle to Bavaro Beach hotel zone ~RD$1,500 ($25), 20 min drive. Pre-book airport transfer or use Uber.

items.title: Check In — Bavaro Beach Hotel
items.detail: Beachfront hotel on Bavaro Beach. Drop bags, change into beach gear. Walking distance to restaurants and beach bars.

items.title: Lunch at Jellyfish Restaurant
items.detail: Beachfront dining on Bavaro Beach. Fresh ceviche, grilled seafood, and Dominican rice and beans. Mains RD$800–1,500 ($14–26). Feet in the sand while you eat.

items.title: Bavaro Beach & Snorkeling
items.detail: Spend the afternoon on one of the Caribbean's most famous beaches. Crystal-clear water, white sand, palm trees. Snorkel gear rental ~RD$500 ($9) from beach vendors.

items.title: Hoyo Azul Cenote at Cap Cana
items.detail: A stunning natural sinkhole with turquoise water at the base of a 75-foot limestone cliff. Swim in the cenote, explore Scape Park. Entry ~RD$3,000 ($50). Book ahead in high season.

items.title: Dinner at Cap Cana Marina
items.detail: Upscale dining at the marina. Try fresh lobster, Dominican-style mofongo, or grilled catch of the day. Budget RD$2,000–4,000/person ($35–70). Sunset views over the marina.

items.title: Breakfast at Hotel
items.detail: Dominican breakfast: mangu (mashed plantains) with fried cheese, salami, and eggs — los tres golpes. Fresh tropical juice and Dominican coffee.

items.title: Shuttle to Santo Domingo
items.detail: Bavaro Express or private transfer to Santo Domingo. ~3 hours via Autopista del Este. Cost: shuttle RD$600–1,000 ($10–17), private transfer ~RD$5,000 ($85). Highway has good rest stops.

items.title: Check In — Zona Colonial Hotel
items.detail: Boutique hotel in the heart of the Colonial Zone. Walking distance to all historic sites, restaurants, and nightlife along Calle El Conde.

items.title: Lunch at Pat'e Palo
items.detail: Historic restaurant in a 16th-century building on Plaza de España. Try the chivo guisado (goat stew) or la bandera dominicana (rice, beans, meat). Mains RD$600–1,200 ($10–20).

items.title: Zona Colonial Walking Tour
items.detail: Explore the first European settlement in the Americas. Visit Alcazar de Colón, Catedral Primada de América (first cathedral in the New World), Fortaleza Ozama, and Calle Las Damas. Self-guided or join a walking tour ~RD$1,500 ($25).

items.title: Malecon Sunset Walk
items.detail: Stroll the Malecon waterfront promenade as the sun sets over the Caribbean. Street vendors, music, and the energy of Dominican nightlife warming up. Stop for a Presidente beer at a beachside bar.

items.title: Dinner at Mesón de Bari
items.detail: Traditional Dominican cuisine in the Colonial Zone. Famous for mofongo (mashed plantains with garlic and pork), sancocho, and fresh seafood. Budget RD$800–1,800/person ($14–30). Live merengue music on weekends.

items.title: Coffee & Mangu at La Cafetería
items.detail: Strong Dominican coffee and fresh mangu with eggs at a local spot in the Colonial Zone. Budget RD$300–500 ($5–9). The best way to start a Dominican morning.

items.title: Los Tres Ojos (Three Eyes)
items.detail: Stunning underground cave system with three crystal-clear lagoons. Descend into the limestone caves and take a boat across the third lagoon. Entry RD$200 ($3.50). Allow 1–1.5 hours.

items.title: Mercado Modelo Shopping
items.detail: Santo Domingo's main artisan market. Larimar jewelry, amber, Dominican cigars, rum, paintings, and souvenirs. Bargain hard — start at half the asking price. Great for last-minute gifts.

items.title: Farewell Lunch — La Bandera Dominicana
items.detail: End the trip with the national dish: la bandera (rice, red beans, and stewed meat) at a local comedor. Budget RD$200–400 ($3.50–7). Simple, satisfying, and authentically Dominican.

items.title: Faro a Colón (Columbus Lighthouse)
items.detail: A massive cross-shaped monument housing Columbus's remains (disputed). The lighthouse projects a cross-shaped beam visible for miles. Museum inside with exhibits on Dominican history and culture. Entry RD$100 ($2).

items.title: Taxi to Las Americas Airport
items.detail: Uber or taxi from Zona Colonial to SDQ airport. ~30 min, RD$1,500–2,000 ($25–35). Allow extra time for traffic. Departure terminal is well-organized.
